topic: From prediction question to the learning table: unit, target, and feature-availability cutoff
scope: The foundation: defining one sample, the target, and index time before making any features.
key_concepts: Before extracting features, we must translate the business/prediction question into a strict learning table structure.
Unit of observation: What exactly is one row? An entity (User), an event (Transaction), or a timestamp (User-Day)?
Target and horizon: What are we predicting, and over what future window?
Index time / Prediction time: The exact point in time when the prediction is made. Any data from after this time is illegal for feature engineering (data leakage).

definitions.definition: The exact entity or event represented by one row in the dataset.
The specific future time window for which the target is being predicted.
The exact moment the model must make its prediction; the cutoff for available features.
Accidentally including information from the future (or the target itself) into the features.

examples: Predicting credit card churn. Unit: Customer-Month. Target: Canceled in the next 30 days. Index time: The last day of the current month. Features can only use data strictly before the index time.

relations.builds_toward: ch02 (where data leakage is the main topic)

exclusions: Do not explain cross-validation or detailed leakage types (e.g. target encoding leakage) here. Just establish the timeline cutoff.
